metadata.title: Why a Particle Can Walk Through a Wall
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = quantum / transmitted wave; CRIMSON = classical / blocked / exponential decay
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no sinh^2 exact formula, no four-region matching algebra, borrowed-energy framing debunked in one beat only. Wonder register: exponential-tail mechanism from first principles, no drills.

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: A ball thrown at a wall it can't clear bounces back every time. A quantum particle sometimes simply appears on the far side — with no energy borrowed, none repaid. How?

card.copy: Why a Particle Can Walk Through a Wall

beats.narration_text: The classical rule is unambiguous: if your energy falls short of the barrier height, you bounce. Always. This isn't an approximation — it's what energy conservation demands.
Same particle, same barrier, same energy — below the barrier height. Classical prediction: total reflection, zero transmission. Yet some fraction passes through. What's happening?

card.copy: Classical physics predicts total reflection when E < V. Here some fraction transmits anyway. Why?

beats.narration_text: The key: the wavefunction doesn't stop at the barrier edge. Instead of hitting a cliff and dying, it begins to decay — exponentially — into the forbidden region.
An exponential never reaches exactly zero. If the barrier is thin enough, a small but nonzero amplitude survives to the far side — and that surviving amplitude launches a real transmitted wave.

production_viz.label: exponential tail surviving to far side, launching transmitted wave

beats.narration_text: The transmitted probability falls off as e to the minus two kappa L — where kappa tells you how forbidden the region is, and L is the barrier's thickness. Double the width and transmission drops exponentially. This is not a small correction.
The particle that emerges carries exactly the same energy it started with. Nothing was borrowed, nothing repaid. It passed through the classically forbidden region — not over it.

production_viz.label: energy unchanged before and after tunneling

beats.narration_text: This is not a theoretical curiosity. Alpha decay from atomic nuclei, hydrogen fusion in the Sun, the imaging of individual atoms with scanning tunneling microscopes — all run on exactly this exponential tail surviving through forbidden regions.

production_viz.label: alpha decay / solar fusion / STM — all run on tunneling

beats.narration_text: Take an electron carrying 1 electron-volt hitting a 5 eV barrier, 5 ångströms wide. Transmission: roughly one in ten thousand. Tiny — but exactly what the exponential formula predicts, and exactly what experiment measures.

The wavefunction leaks because exponentials never reach zero. That leak is tunneling — not magic, not energy borrowed from nowhere. It's what waves do when they enter a region they classically cannot occupy.

card.copy: Exponentials never reach zero. The leak is the tunnel.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
